deep_dive.title: A Physics-Grounded Benchmark for Multi-Agent Dynamics in World Models
deep_dive.abstract: Generative world models hold immense promise as scalable simulators for autonomous systems, particularly for synthesizing rare but safety-critical multi-agent interactions, such as vehicle collisions. However, current evaluation paradigms index heavily on visual fidelity and semantic alignment, leaving a critical blind spot: they cannot reliably quantify whether generated dynamics actually obey the fundamental physical laws required for reliable simulation. Assessing this physical plausibility is inherently difficult due to a lack of physical metrics and the challenge of extracting metric-scale kinematics from uncalibrated video rollouts. To bridge this gap, we introduce CrashTwin, a physics-grounded evaluation framework designed to stress-test the physical trustworthiness of world models. CrashTwin couples a diverse dataset of multi-agent collision scenarios, comprising 25K controllable synthetic and 12K in-the-wild real-world collision sequences with a novel calibration-free reconstruction pipeline, enabling the recovery of 3D physical attributes directly from world model rollouts. We propose a diagnostic suite that systematically evaluates three dimensions: spatio-temporal consistency, momentum and kinetic energy conservation, and world-dynamics integrity. Extensive benchmarking of state-of-the-art models reveals a crucial insight: high perceptual quality frequently masks severe physical violations during complex interactions. By quantitatively exposing these failure modes, CrashTwin provides a vital diagnostic tool for developing physically grounded world models capable of reliable real-world simulation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: physics-grounded benchmark for evaluating generated video dynamics
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于物理的生成视频动力学评估基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于物理的生成视频动力学评估基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于物理的生成视频动力学评估基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CrashTwin introduces a physics-grounded benchmark to quantify physical plausibility of generated multi-agent dynamics, filling a gap in video generation evaluation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前生成视频评估过度依赖视觉保真度，忽略了物理合理性。CrashTwin提出了一个基于物理的评估框架，通过提取视频中的运动学指标来量化生成动态是否遵循物理定律。实验表明该框架能有效检测出违反物理规则的生成样本，为视频生成模型提供了更可靠的评估维度。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 当前生成视频评估过度依赖视觉保真度，忽略了物理合理性。CrashTwin提出了一个基于物理的评估框架，通过提取视频中的运动学指标来量化生成动态是否遵循物理定律。实验表明该框架能有效检测出违反物理规则的生成样本，为视频生成模型提供了更可靠的评估维度。

quick_skim.title: Your Data Manifold is Secretly a Reward Model: Shell-LCC for Text-to-Video Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Recent text-to-video (T2V) diffusion models rely heavily on auxiliary reward signals (e.g., via reward models or DPO) to align generated content with human aesthetics and improve realism. These signals, however, incur substantial computational overhead, require costly human annotations, and often yield limited improvement in fine-grained local details. In this paper, we argue that your data manifold is secretly a reward model. By explicitly modeling the manifold structure of high-quality Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) data and encouraging video latents to lie on this manifold, we derive dense, differentiable, and nearly cost-free reward signals that significantly improve video quality, particularly in mitigating low-level distortions. Our modeling builds upon Local Coordinate Coding (LCC), which captures the `skeleton' of the manifold. However, directly applying LCC suffers from mean regression, pulling latents toward the geometric mean and losing high-frequency details. We therefore extend it to Shell Local Coordinate Coding (Shell-LCC), which models the manifold `surface' as an isotropic shell to align with the true high-density region. Experiments demonstrate that our approach improves realism, enhances high-frequency details, reduces over-smoothing artifacts, and alleviates motion blur.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses data manifold as reward to improve video quality
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用数据流形作为奖励信号提升视频生成质量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用数据流形作为奖励信号提升视频生成质量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用数据流形作为奖励信号提升视频生成质量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Shell-LCC which treats data manifold as a reward model to improve text-to-video quality without extra annotations, addressing evaluation of generated videos.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出Shell-LCC方法，针对文本到视频生成中奖励信号计算开销大、需要人工标注的问题，将高质量数据流形作为隐式奖励模型，通过鼓励视频潜变量位于该流形上来生成密集、可微且几乎零成本的奖励信号。实验表明该方法能显著提升视频质量，特别是减少低级失真。该工作提供了一种新的视频质量评价思路，将数据流形与奖励模型等价。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出Shell-LCC方法，针对文本到视频生成中奖励信号计算开销大、需要人工标注的问题，将高质量数据流形作为隐式奖励模型，通过鼓励视频潜变量位于该流形上来生成密集、可微且几乎零成本的奖励信号。实验表明该方法能显著提升视频质量，特别是减少低级失真。该工作提供了一种新的视频质量评价思路，将数据流形与奖励模型等价。

quick_skim.title: Animation2Code: Evaluating Temporal Visual Reasoning in Video-to-Code Generation
quick_skim.abstract: While recent vision-language models (VLMs) have achieved significant improvements on static visual-to-code tasks such as generating code for webpages, charts, or SVGs, it remains unclear whether they can recover temporal dynamics when motion is present. To this end, we introduce Animation2Code, a benchmark for evaluating temporal visual reasoning via reconstructing executable web animation code from videos. Animation2Code consists of 1,069 web animation videos with diverse visual appearances and motion patterns, paired with corresponding HTML/CSS/JavaScript implementations. We propose two human-aligned metrics, appearance similarity and temporal similarity, which allow us to disentangle visual fidelity from temporal alignment when comparing rendered animations against ground-truth samples. Benchmarking state-of-the-art VLMs on this dataset shows that current VLMs struggle to maintain temporal consistency in reconstruction, even when achieving high appearance similarity, including under finetuning and iterative refinement settings. Code and data are available at https://anya-ji.github.io/animation2code-website .
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes two human-aligned metrics (appearance similarity and temporal similarity) for evaluating video quality
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出两个人类对齐指标（外观相似性和时间相似性）用于评估视频质量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出两个人类对齐指标（外观相似性和时间相似性）用于评估视频质量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出两个人类对齐指标（外观相似性和时间相似性）用于评估视频质量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Animation2Code introduces a benchmark for evaluating temporal visual reasoning in video-to-code generation and proposes appearance similarity and temporal similarity metrics that can be repurposed for generated video quality assessment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 论文针对视频到代码生成任务中时间视觉推理评估的缺失，构建了包含1069个网页动画视频的基准数据集，并提出外观相似性和时间相似性两个人类对齐指标。这两个指标分别衡量视觉保真度和时间对齐，可迁移至生成视频的质量评估，为视频生成评价提供了新的度量工具。实验表明该基准能有效区分模型在动态内容上的表现。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 论文针对视频到代码生成任务中时间视觉推理评估的缺失，构建了包含1069个网页动画视频的基准数据集，并提出外观相似性和时间相似性两个人类对齐指标。这两个指标分别衡量视觉保真度和时间对齐，可迁移至生成视频的质量评估，为视频生成评价提供了新的度量工具。实验表明该基准能有效区分模型在动态内容上的表现。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: video quality assessment methods for generated videos

quick_skim.title: DTI: Dynamic Trajectory Initialization for Generative Face Video Super-Resolution
quick_skim.abstract: As the most perceptually powerful Face Video Super-Resolution (FVSR) method, existing works in Generative FVSR (GFVSR) mainly exploit the generative prior of pretrained diffusion models. However, viewed as full generation, they suffer from fixed sampling and expensive inference costs if without large-scale auxiliary training. Furthermore, an excessive pursuit of generic perceptual metrics often results in low fidelity. To address these issues, we present Dynamic Trajectory Initialization (DTI) paradigm for GFVSR, which reformulates GFVSR as an input-driven directional restoration. With a novel enhancement-and-injection conditioning mechanism for pretrained DiT backbone, fidelity of our model has been significantly improved without compromising perceptual quality. To dynamically set the starting sampling point, we propose a Discriminative Guide (DG) trained via objective Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) alignment. With only minor model adaptation and fine-tuning, our method achieves a SOTA overall performance across diverse metrics and benchmarks. An analysis of relationship between actual comprehensive quality and common metrics is also conducted, which demonstrates the perception-distortion trade-off and that the LPIPS is the most convincing metric in our case.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: discusses limitations of generic perceptual metrics and proposes method for fidelity
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 讨论了通用感知指标的局限性并提出提升保真度的方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 讨论了通用感知指标的局限性并提出提升保真度的方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 讨论了通用感知指标的局限性并提出提升保真度的方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Critiques generic perceptual metrics for video super-resolution and proposes Dynamic Trajectory Initialization to improve fidelity without compromising perceptual quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对生成式人脸视频超分中过度追求通用感知指标导致保真度低的问题，提出动态轨迹初始化（DTI）范式，将超分任务重构为输入驱动的定向恢复。通过增强注入条件机制和预训练DiT骨干，在不牺牲感知质量的前提下显著提升了保真度。该工作揭示了现有感知指标的局限性，为视频生成评价指标的设计提供了重要参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对生成式人脸视频超分中过度追求通用感知指标导致保真度低的问题，提出动态轨迹初始化（DTI）范式，将超分任务重构为输入驱动的定向恢复。通过增强注入条件机制和预训练DiT骨干，在不牺牲感知质量的前提下显著提升了保真度。该工作揭示了现有感知指标的局限性，为视频生成评价指标的设计提供了重要参考。
